REPORTS FROM SOCIAL MEDIA AND EAST IDAHO NEWS OF 15 VEHICLES WITH SHATTERED WINDOWS, AND ANOTHER 20-30 VEHICLES WITH WIND-DRIVEN ROCK DAMAGE AT TADD JENKINS CHEVROLET ALONG FARNSWORTH WAY. MANY OF THE VEHICLES DENTED. PRELIMINARY DAMAGE ESTIMATE BY BUSINESS $10,000 TO $20,000. TIME ESTIMATED BASED ON RADAR.

PINE TREE SNAPPED OFF NEAR THE BASE ALONG E RIRIE HIGHWAY EAST OF HILLSIDE DR. ALSO TREE LIMBS AND FENCES DOWN IN TOWN ALONG 2ND ST W, BUT NO MAJOR DAMAGE THERE. TIME ESTIMATED BASED ON RADAR.
MULTIPLE ZERO VISIBILITY REPORTS FROM BLOWING DUST ALONG INTERSTATE 86 FROM 1 MILE WEST OF ARBON VALLEY EXIT TO 1 MILE WEST OF RAINBOW ROAD EXIT.

BLOWING DUST RESULTED IN ZERO VISIBILITY AND NUMEROUS PILEUPS ALONG AN 8-MILE STRETCH OF INTERSTATE 86 BETWEEN THE SEAGULL BAY EXIT AND THE ARBON VALLEY EXIT BETWEEN THE POCATELLO AIRPORT AND AMERICAN FALLS. THE ACCIDENTS OCCURRED BETWEEN 11:30AM AND 12:30PM AND THIS NECESSITATED THE CLOSURE. SEVERAL PERSONAL VEHICLES AND SEMI TRUCKS WERE INVOLVED IN THE INCIDENTS. SEVERAL INJURIES WERE REPORTED AND THESE PATIENTS WERE TRANSPORTED TO POCATELLOS HOSPITAL. THE SEVERITY OF THE INJURIES IS UNKNOWN AS WELL AS THE NUMBER OF INJURIES. ONE OF THE SEMIS WAS CARRYING A LOAD OF FERTILIZER AND THIS FERTILIZER SPILLED AS A RESULT OF THE CRASH. HAZMAT CREWS WERE CALLED IN TO RESPOND TO THIS CHEMICAL SPILL. IDAHO STATE POLICE DESCRIBED THE VISIBILITY AS A BLACKOUT WITH ZERO VISIBILITY. THE INTERSTATE ULTIMATELY RE-OPENED AROUND 9PM.
2 PUBLIC REPORTS OF NICKEL TO QUARTER SIZE HAIL FALLING IN DIETRICH. TIME ESTIMATED BASED ON REPORTS AND RADAR.

A LARGE TREE WENT DOWN AT MAIN AND SILVER IN DOWNTOWN HAILEY. MAIN ST BLOCKED AND TRAFFIC DIVERTED ROUGHLY ONE HOUR. ANECDOTAL CONVERSATION WITH WITNESS SAID IT HIT SOUTHBOUND VEHICLE ACROSS HOOD. VISIBILITY WAS LESS THAN 1/10 MILE DURING WIND EVENT FOR 2-3 MINUTES IN DOWNTOWN HAILEY DUE TO DUST.
STRONG WINDS ESTIMATED AT 60 MPH WITH MINOR DAMAGE TO UNSECURED OBJECTS. NO RAIN OR HAIL AS WINDS CAME THROUGH.
